skill.name: code-explainer
skill.description: Explains complex code in clear, audience-specific language for onboarding, reviews, documentation, and shared team understanding.
skill.summary: Turn complex code into clear explanations for different technical audiences.
skill.author: CuriousLearner

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, network, environment access, sensitive data, and blocker patterns across SKILL.md. Manual review found these are Markdown templates, diagrams, educational code snippets, and documentation links rather than executable skill behavior. No evidence found of prompt injection, malicious intent, real command execution, credential access, or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Command Execution
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives. The flagged locations are Markdown fences, inline code, diagrams, and JavaScript examples used to show explanation formats; SKILL.md contains no executable script, tool wrapper, shell command runner, or instruction to execute user input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown-only and the flagged backticks are part of documentation examples. There is no surrounding executable runtime or command invocation path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Indicators Are Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: The network findings are false positives. The Python HTTP library and hardcoded URL detections occur in explanatory prose, a public documentation link, and a sample configuration object; none initiate network traffic from the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows the URLs and network terms are static documentation content. No fetch, request execution, webhook, or exfiltration destination is present as skill behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Environment and Secret References Are Sample Authentication Code
low_findings.description: The environment access and sensitive data findings are false positives. The flagged process.env and JWT secret references appear inside an example login walkthrough that teaches authentication concepts and does not read real environment variables.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The references are inside fenced JavaScript examples and explanatory bullets. There is no executable adapter or instruction for the assistant to collect, print, or transmit secrets.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Blocker Keywords Are Educational Security Content
low_findings.description: The C2, weak cryptography, browser storage, and reconnaissance-style detections are false positives in context. They appear in JWT diagrams, security notes, code review exercises, and discussions of common pitfalls rather than instructions to perform harmful activity.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious terms are embedded in defensive explanations and review prompts. No malicious workflow, persistence mechanism, reconnaissance procedure, or prompt injection text was found.

content.user_title: Explain Code Clearly for Any Team
content.value_statement: Complex code slows onboarding, reviews, and maintenance when teams lack shared context. This skill turns code into clear walkthroughs, diagrams, examples, and audience-specific explanations.
actual_capabilities: Explains code purpose, responsibilities, dependencies, and execution flow.
Adapts explanations for junior, mid-level, senior, and non-technical audiences.
Creates step-by-step walkthroughs with key concepts and common pitfalls.
Suggests diagrams, learning exercises, and documentation sections for code understanding.
Highlights security, performance, testing, and architecture considerations in reviewed code.
limitations: It depends on the code and context provided by the user or workspace.
It does not execute or verify code behavior by itself.
It may need project-specific background to explain business intent accurately.
It can miss runtime issues that require logs, tests, or production data.

use_cases.title: Onboard New Developers
use_cases.description: Create clear walkthroughs of services, modules, and flows so new team members understand code faster.

use_cases.title: Prepare Code Review Context
use_cases.description: Summarize how a change works, why it matters, and what reviewers should examine.

use_cases.title: Document Complex Systems
use_cases.description: Turn implementation details into readable documentation with diagrams, examples, and audience-specific sections.

prompt_templates.title: Explain One File
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner explanation for a single source file
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain this file for a junior developer. Cover its purpose, main functions, data flow, and any confusing parts.

prompt_templates.title: Walk Through a Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate explanation of a feature path
prompt_templates.prompt: Walk through how this feature works from request to response. Include key files, important decisions, and likely failure points.

prompt_templates.title: Create Team Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Convert code understanding into durable documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create documentation for this module. Include an overview, responsibilities, dependencies, sequence diagram text, common pitfalls, and maintenance notes.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Architecture Tradeoffs
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain the architecture of this code for senior engineers. Focus on design patterns, scalability, security, observability, and tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: A payment service with order lookup, payment capture, database update, and confirmation email.
output_examples.output: A structured walkthrough describing the service purpose, request flow, payment sequence, failure handling, security concerns, and improvement opportunities.

output_examples.input: An authentication middleware that reads a token, verifies it, and attaches user data to a request.
output_examples.output: An audience-specific explanation covering what the middleware does, why ordering matters, how errors are returned, and what token handling risks exist.

output_examples.input: A confusing asynchronous function that returns before several operations finish.
output_examples.output: A plain-language bug explanation with the execution timeline, root cause, corrected approach, and practice questions for learners.

best_practices: Provide the relevant files, target audience, and reason for the explanation.
Ask for both what the code does and why the design was chosen.
Request diagrams or step-by-step flow when explaining multi-file behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for broad explanations without sharing the code or context.
Do not use generated explanations as a substitute for tests or production validation.
Do not ignore security or error-handling notes in the explanation.

faq.question: What does this skill help explain?
faq.answer: It helps explain source code, feature flows, architecture choices, dependencies, security concerns, and common pitfalls.

faq.question: Can it explain code for non-technical readers?
faq.answer: Yes. It can simplify technical details and focus on business behavior, inputs, outputs, and system impact.

faq.question: Does it run the code?
faq.answer: No. It creates explanations from provided code and context. Separate tests or runtime checks are still needed.

faq.question: Can it create documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can draft overviews, walkthroughs, diagrams in text form, README sections, and learning exercises.

faq.question: How should I get the best result?
faq.answer: Provide the files, describe the audience, and state whether you need a quick summary or a detailed walkthrough.

faq.question: Is it useful during code review?
faq.answer: Yes. It can summarize intent, explain control flow, identify important review areas, and highlight risks.
